name: Pallof Press
resistance_profile: Anti-rotation core exercise: the cable pulls you into a twist and you resist it — trains the obliques and deep core to brace against rotation.
cues: Stand side-on to the cable, hands at your chest
Press straight out and resist the twist
Brace the abs; don't let your torso rotate
execution_steps: Set a cable to about chest height and stand side-on to the stack.
Hold the handle with both hands at your chest, then step away to put tension on the cable.
Brace your abs and press the handle straight out in front of your chest.
Resist the cable's pull to twist you, keeping your torso square and still.
Return the handle to your chest under control, finish your reps, then face the other way.
good_when: You want to train the core to resist rotation (anti-rotation stability).
You're a beginner building a braced, stable trunk.
You want a low-back-friendly core exercise with easy load progression.
bad_when: You're after direct ab growth or visible size (use crunches or leg raises).
No cable or resistance band is available.
You want dynamic rotational power (use a woodchop).
common_errors: Letting the torso rotate toward the cable
Using the arms instead of bracing the core
regressions: Hands held closer to the chest (shorter lever)
